title: Slow Cooked Indian Chicken Thighs Tagine
description: A flavorful and tender one-pot Indian dish featuring chicken thighs, cooked to perfection in a tagine, with earthy spices and aromas. This halal-friendly recipe is perfect for a special occasion or a cozy night in.
instructions: Heat oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken thighs and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the ground cumin, ground coriander, turmeric, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Pour in the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Cover the tagine or Dutch oven with a lid and transfer it to the oven.
Bake at 300 degrees Fahrenheit for 30 minutes, then reduce the heat to 275 degrees Fahrenheit and continue cooking for an additional 60 minutes.
Remove the tagine or Dutch oven from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Serve the chicken and sauce over rice or with naan bread.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
